title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. brachyphylla
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. brachyphylla, also known as the short-leaved marsh orchid, is a subspecies of the orchid family (Orchidaceae) native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza incarnata var. brachyphylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and alleviate various health issues.
